name: Ruta de los Puentes Trail
name_original: Parque Natural Saja-Besaya – trail run circular por la Ruta de los Puentes
description: Jogging the difficult 9.7-mile Ruta de los Puentes Trail in Parque Natural Saja-Besaya offers 1267 feet of elevation gain in under two hours
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ruente traverse the Saja Valley, characterized by green mountains, lush forests, and open meadows. A significant portion of the municipality lies within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, offering diverse terrain for running. The landscape features include flat river plains along the Saja River, dense oak and beech forests, and higher plateaus such as El Moral. This varied topography provides a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around Ruente The most popular jogging route is Ruta de los Puentes Trail, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is La Fuentona de Ruente – running circular by the Saja river, a moderate 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This route follows the Saja River and passes by the notable La Fuentona spring. Local runners also love the Running loop from Sopeña, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Jogging around Ruente is defined by green mountains, dense forests, and river plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths along rivers to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Ruente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Ruente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Ruente?
A: There are over 60 running routes available in Ruente, offering a wide range of experiences from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain trails. These routes traverse the beautiful Saja Valley, including parts of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Ruente?
A: Yes, Ruente offers several easier options. You'll find around 6 routes classified as easy, perfect for beginners or a relaxed run. A great moderate option that follows the Saja River and passes by the notable La Fuentona spring is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999266144/la-fuentona-de-ruente-running-circular-by-the-saja-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona de Ruente – running circular by the Saja river&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Ruente?
A: The running trails in Ruente offer a diverse range of terrain. You can expect everything from flat river plains along the Saja River to dense oak and beech forests, and even higher plateaus like El Moral, providing varied challenges and scenic views for all types of runners.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a longer challenge, Ruente has around 34 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999138227/ruta-de-los-puentes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Puentes Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.7 miles (15.6 km) with significant elevation gain, offering a demanding and rewarding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Ruente?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Ruente are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999266144/la-fuentona-de-ruente-running-circular-by-the-saja-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona de Ruente – running circular by the Saja river&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along the Saja River.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while jogging in Ruente?
A: Ruente's trails offer access to several natural and historical landmarks. You can explore areas near the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt; with its panoramic views, or discover the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt;. The emblematic La Fuentona spring and a medieval bridge are also notable sights along some routes.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: While not directly listed in the guide's routes, the region research indicates that the Lamiña Waterfalls are located near Barcenillas, nestled within a beautiful beech forest. This area offers picturesque scenery and could be incorporated into a run for a refreshing experience.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Ruente?
A: The running routes in Ruente are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from numerous reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes in Ruente provide stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via a forest track, offers amazing landscapes and panoramic views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3392395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Esteban Hermitage (Monte Corona)&lt;/a&gt; is another spot known for its beautiful vistas, often found along forest tracks.

Q: Are there running trails through dense forests in Ruente?
A: Yes, Ruente is home to the Monte Aá oak forest, one of the best oak groves in northern Spain, covering over 400 hectares. This dense forest provides shaded trails and a beautiful natural setting for running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sopeña&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a trail leading through forested areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ruente?
A: Ruente's green mountains, lush forests, and open meadows make it an excellent destination for running throughout much of the year. The varied topography provides options for different seasons; shaded forest trails are pleasant in warmer months, while river plains offer accessible routes in cooler weather. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.
tips: The Route of the Bridges itinerary offers a wonderful circular tour through the heart of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, being able to appreciate its picturesque landscape that varies depending on the time you visit it.

The Oak "Tumbao" is one of the trees cataloged within "singular trees of Cantabria" and it is a "Cajiga" (Quercus Robur) about 25 meters high and approximately 250 years old, its curiosity is that one day full of strong wind blew snow on its branches and the tree fell because it could not withstand so much weight, but instead of being its end and by keeping some of its roots rooted in the ground, it was able to survive and new branches sprouted. Today 4 of its branches are 4 rootless trees formed from the original trunk and filling Brujilices with beauty, within the Vados River Forest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ruta de los Puentes Trail?
faqs.answer: The Ruta de los Puentes Trail is rated as difficult for jogging. It covers a distance of approximately 15.5 km with an elevation gain of about 386 meters, making it a challenging route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail follows the course of a river, featuring diverse terrain that includes riverbanks and natural landscapes. You'll encounter several bridges, some with historical significance, and potentially some ruins. The path is generally well-defined but can be demanding due to its length and elevation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is known for its numerous bridges, giving it its name. You'll also pass by the Puentes Trail itself, and the impressive Fallen Oak of Brujilices. The trail offers picturesque river views and historical elements throughout.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de los Puentes Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Parque Natural Saja-Besaya, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the trail or the Parque Natural Saja-Besaya?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Ruta de los Puentes Trail or the Parque Natural Saja-Besaya for day use. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or special requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, especially to experience the lush greenery of areas like the pradera de Navalagrulla. The weather is generally milder, and the natural scenery is at its peak. Autumn also offers beautiful colors, but be mindful of potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where does the Ruta de los Puentes Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking details are not provided, trails of this nature often have designated parking areas near their starting points. It's recommended to check local maps or komoot's tour details for the most precise starting location and parking options before you go.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route extensively follows the 'Ruta de los puentes' (100% of the route) and also passes through significant sections of the 'Sendero de los Puentes' (46%). Smaller portions intersect with 'Ruta del Hayacorva' (23%) and 'Camino del Haya Corva'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's 'difficult' rating and length, it's essential to bring comfortable and appropriate jogging shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and weather-appropriate clothing. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as well as a first-aid kit.

faqs.question: Are there any specific natural features or viewpoints to look out for?
faqs.answer: Beyond the numerous bridges, the trail offers picturesque riverbanks and diverse natural landscapes. A particularly beautiful spot is noted near the El Chico bridge. The route is rich in flora and fauna, especially in spring, providing many scenic viewpoints along the river's course.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de los Puentes Trail suitable for families with young children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, approximately 15.5 km distance, and 386 meters of elevation gain, this trail is generally not recommended for families with young children or those seeking an easy, leisurely walk. It's better suited for experienced joggers or hikers.
